analysis.mechanism: Loss aversion: potential losses loom larger than equivalent gains, so explicitly framing waiting as lossless reduces pressure to act (Kahneman & Tversky, 1979).
analysis.mechanism_source: Kahneman, D., & Tversky, A. (1979). Prospect theory: An analysis of decision under risk. Econometrica, 47(2), 263-291.
analysis.current_issue: EN and all three translations preserve the same loss-neutral permission: waiting does not forfeit anything.

analysis.mechanism: Sunk-cost pressure: ownership can create a felt need to justify prior acquisition, so separating possession from obligation reduces backlog debt (Arkes & Blumer, 1985).
analysis.mechanism_source: Arkes, H. R., & Blumer, C. (1985). The psychology of sunk cost. Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes, 35(1), 124-140.
analysis.current_issue: EN and all three translations consistently detach ownership from duty. The permission remains pure and nonjudgmental.

analysis.mechanism: Intention-superiority effect: unfulfilled intentions stay cognitively active, so permission works best when intention is decoupled from execution rather than from generic 'input' (Goschke & Kuhl, 1993).
analysis.mechanism_source: Goschke, T., & Kuhl, J. (1993). Representation of intentions: Persisting activation in memory. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, and Cognition, 19(5), 1211-1226.
analysis.current_issue: EN kernel/interface shift from intention to 'input' and 'execution,' which weakens the target mechanism. All translations inherit that mismatch.

en.interface: You may intend to play without acting on it.

zh-TW.interface: 你想玩，不需要立刻採取行動。

zh-CN.interface: 你想玩，不需要立刻采取行动。

ja.interface: 遊びたいと思うだけで、行動は不要です。

analysis.mechanism: FOMO and scarcity salience: perceived diminishing availability increases pressure to act, so stating that availability does not decay reduces urgency (Przybylski et al., 2013).
analysis.mechanism_source: Przybylski, A. K., Murayama, K., DeHaan, C. R., & Gladwell, V. (2013). Motivational, emotional, and behavioral correlates of fear of missing out. Computers in Human Behavior, 29(4), 1841-1848.
analysis.current_issue: EN and all three translations preserve the same anti-scarcity meaning: waiting does not reduce availability.

analysis.mechanism: Temporal scarcity: when time feels scarce, attention narrows and pressure increases, so permission should neutralize urgency cues rather than imply literal system-time suspension (Shah et al., 2012).
analysis.mechanism_source: Shah, A. K., Mullainathan, S., & Shafir, E. (2012). Some consequences of having too little. Science, 338(6107), 682-685.
analysis.current_issue: EN overstates the claim with 'System time is suspended,' and all translations mirror that literal, implausible framing. The mechanism is reduced time pressure, not paused clock time.

en.interface: No time is running out on you while you wait.

zh-TW.interface: 你等待時，沒有任何時間正在耗盡。

zh-CN.interface: 你等待时，没有任何时间正在耗尽。

ja.kernel: 時間圧は作動していません。
ja.interface: 待っているあいだ、あなたの時間は減っていません。

analysis.mechanism: Status quo bias: maintaining a non-choice default is psychologically easier than selecting an option, so validating non-selection as stable reduces action pressure (Samuelson & Zeckhauser, 1988).
analysis.mechanism_source: Samuelson, W., & Zeckhauser, R. (1988). Status quo bias in decision making. Journal of Risk and Uncertainty, 1(1), 7-59.
analysis.current_issue: EN and all three translations clearly validate non-selection as an acceptable stable state. Mechanism alignment is strong.

analysis.mechanism: Choice overload and assortment pressure: larger sets can increase decision difficulty and felt burden, so permission should neutralize the library's demand signal directly (Iyengar & Lepper, 2000).
analysis.mechanism_source: Iyengar, S. S., & Lepper, M. R. (2000). When choice is demotivating: Can one desire too much of a good thing? Journal of Personality and Social Psychology, 79(6), 995-1006.
analysis.current_issue: EN kernel 'Database is passive' is too technical and indirect; it weakens the backlog/library mechanism. All translations follow that abstraction, though the interface lines are closer.

en.interface: Your library is not asking anything of you.

zh-TW.interface: 你的遊戲庫不會對你提出任何要求。

zh-CN.interface: 你的游戏库不会对你提出任何要求。

ja.kernel: ライブラリの規模は中立です。
ja.interface: あなたのライブラリは、何も求めていません。

analysis.mechanism: Opportunity-cost salience: when waiting feels costly, pressure to choose rises, so explicitly zeroing the perceived cost protects inaction (Frederick et al., 2009).
analysis.mechanism_source: Frederick, S., Novemsky, N., Wang, J., Dhar, R., & Nowlis, S. (2009). Opportunity cost neglect. Journal of Consumer Research, 36(4), 553-561.
analysis.current_issue: EN and all three translations keep the intended meaning intact: waiting carries no penalty. The framing stays administrative rather than advisory.

analysis.mechanism: Scarcity/FOMO reduction: stable future availability lowers urgency, but the kernel should name availability directly rather than abstract 'access' as static (Przybylski et al., 2013).
analysis.mechanism_source: Przybylski, A. K., Murayama, K., DeHaan, C. R., & Gladwell, V. (2013). Motivational, emotional, and behavioral correlates of fear of missing out. Computers in Human Behavior, 29(4), 1841-1848.
analysis.current_issue: EN kernel 'Access is static' is semantically stiff and the zh-TW/zh-CN kernels sound unnatural. The interface meaning is mostly correct, but all four can align more directly to persistent availability.

en.interface: What is available to you stays available.

zh-TW.interface: 現在可用的，之後也仍然可用。

zh-CN.interface: 现在可用的，之后也仍然可用。

ja.kernel: 利用可能性は変化しません。
ja.interface: 今使えるものは、そのまま使えます。

analysis.mechanism: Action bias: people often feel pressure to signal movement or readiness, so permission should legitimize continued pause without requiring proof of readiness (Patt & Zeckhauser, 2000).
analysis.mechanism_source: Patt, A., & Zeckhauser, R. (2000). Action bias and environmental decisions. Journal of Risk and Uncertainty, 21(1), 45-72.
analysis.current_issue: EN kernel/interface are conceptually close but phrased opaquely; all translations inherit that opacity. The mechanism is clearer when the pause itself is validated and readiness-signaling is explicitly unnecessary.

en.interface: You do not need to signal readiness.

zh-TW.interface: 你不需要發出任何準備好的訊號。

zh-CN.interface: 你不需要发出任何准备好的信号。

ja.kernel: 一時停止は有効なままです。
ja.interface: 準備ができたことを示す必要はありません。
